member.name: Anneliese Midgley
member.synopsis: Anneliese Midgley is the Labour MP for Knowsley , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Postal Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the hon. Member for Warrington North, representing the Speaker's Committee for the Independent Parliamentary Standards Authority, what information he holds on the total amount claimed by hon. Members for postage costs through IPSA in each year sinc
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Independent Parliamentary Standards Authority (IPSA) has provided the total aggregated spend on postage costs across all MPs in the table below.Financial YearSpend2015-16£185,9312016-17£101,5722017-18£163,6912018-19£150,7232019-20£167,1452020-21£296,2...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Speaker's Committee for the Independent Parliamentary Standards Authority

writtenQuestions.heading: Fly-tipping
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many enforcement notices for illegal waste operations are outstanding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the last 5 years (1 January 2021 – 31 December 2025) there have been 73 notices served under Section 59 and Section 34 of Environmental Protection Act 1990. A Section 59 notice requires the removal of unlawfully deposited waste, and a Section 34 notice...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Fly-tipping
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many illegal waste site operators have received custodial sentences in each of the last five years.

writtenQuestions.heading: Fly-tipping
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many prosecutions has the Environment Agency brought for operating illegal waste sites in each of the last five years.

writtenQuestions.heading: Fly-tipping
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what estimate her Department has made of the total volume of illegally stored or dumped waste currently present at illegal waste sites in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Total tonnage of waste on illegal waste sites that were operational on or after 1 January 2021 to 31 December 2025C (inclusive) and have not been brought into regulation or cleared of waste: 5,544,462. NB: This data is taken from a live system and maybe s...

writtenQuestions.heading: Fly-tipping
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what estimate her Department has made of the total number of illegal waste dumps currently operating in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Total number of illegal waste sites operating as of 17 March 2026: 537. NB: This data is taken from a live system and maybe subject to change as investigation details are updated.

writtenQuestions.heading: Fly-tipping

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Homicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, ??what recent discussions she has had with the Domestic Abuse Commissioner on (a) the timing of launching Domestic Homicide Reviews and (b) the implementation of recommendations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Taking learnings seriously following domestic abuse related deaths is paramount. Domestic Homicide Reviews (DHRs) offer a vital opportunity for national and local agencies, local communities and society as a whole to learn lessons from domestic abuse rela...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Homicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, ??what mechanisms are in place to monitor compliance by local areas with statutory guidance on the timely commissioning and completion of Domestic Homicide Reviews.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Domestic homicide reviews (DHR) offer a vital opportunity for national and local agencies, local communities and society as a whole to learn lessons from domestic abuse related deaths and treat every death as preventable. However, whilst the Government re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Homicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that Domestic Homicide Reviews are launched as soon as possible.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Domestic homicide reviews (DHR) offer a vital opportunity for national and local agencies, local communities and society as a whole to learn lessons from domestic abuse related deaths and treat every death as preventable. However, whilst the Government re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Homicide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many Domestic Homicide Reviews were not commissioned within the statutory timescale in each of the last five years; and what the longest recorded time taken was in that period.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Epilepsy: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of his Department's funding for epilepsy research.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds research into epilepsy via the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). Between 2020/21 and 2024/25, the NIHR invested £12.8 million in direct research funding on epilepsy. This investment in epilepsy research allows us...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: GCE A-level: Knowsley
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many 16 year olds study A-levels after GCSEs in each ward in Knowsley.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department publishes data on entry and attainment for students at the end of 16 to 18 study. For the 2024/25 academic year, the latest for which data is available, 79 students in schools or colleges in Knowsley were at the end of 16 to 18 study, of wh...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
